operating_state: Net sales declined 3.0% to $37.7 billion driven by negative comparable sales of 3.1%, reflecting 2.7% decrease in customer transactions and 0.3% decline in average ticket. Transaction decline reflects macroeconomic pressures and moderating home improvement demand, while average ticket was impacted by commodity price deflation offset partially by new product demand. Building Materials posted positive comparable sales while other merchandising departments declined, with Lumber experiencing double-digit declines from price deflation.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed to 33.8% from 34.0% primarily due to price stabilization partially offset by lower supply chain costs. SG&A increased to 17.6% of sales from 16.6% driven by deleverage from negative comparable sales and previously executed wage investments for hourly associates. Depreciation and amortization rose to 1.8% from 1.6% reflecting ongoing business investments and sales deleverage.
material_events: The Board promoted Ann-Marie Campbell to Senior Executive Vice President effective November 1, 2023, expanding her responsibilities to include outside pro sales and installation services while maintaining oversight of U.S. stores and international operations. The company completed a $2.0 billion debt offering in December 2023 with notes maturing in 2025, 2026, and 2029. The Board increased quarterly dividend 10% to $2.09 per share and approved a new $15.0 billion share repurchase authorization.
macro_exposures: Macroeconomic factors contributed to decreased customer transactions and moderating home improvement demand, representing ongoing pressures rather than new risk escalations. No material changes in macro risk exposures were disclosed during the period.
forward_outlook: Management plans approximately $3 billion in capital expenditures for fiscal 2023, representing approximately 2% of net sales annually. The company maintains disciplined capital allocation approach prioritizing business investment, dividend payments, and returning excess cash through share repurchases, with flexibility to adjust capital expenditures based on economic conditions.
uncertainty_profile: The company maintains standard commercial paper and credit facilities totaling $5.0 billion with no material covenant restrictions expected to impact liquidity. No material changes to critical accounting estimates or significant accounting policies occurred during the period.
